Title: Push install on Local Office agent (x64) - happy path
Pre-requisites: Clean target machine (no agent). Admin/domain credentials configured in server. WMI reachable; ports open between server and target.
Steps:
1. Pre-clean target machine (see cleanup TC).
2. In console: Agent > Scope of Management > Computers > Add Computers; add domain/workgroup with admin credentials. [Qengine browser]
3. Select the target from the discovered list and click Install Agents. [Qengine browser]
4. Server copies installer over the network using admin credentials and runs it silently.
1. Record target architecture: run `wmic os get osarchitecture` (use $env:PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE for ARM64). [run_command]
2. In UEMS console (Agent > Scope of Management > Computers) confirm the machine is listed under the correct office with remark "Agent Installed successfully" / status Active. [Qengine browser]
3. Verify service "ManageEngine UEMS Agent Service" exists and is RUNNING. [service_actions: status -> running]
4. Verify startup type Automatic: HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\..\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\DCAgent -> Start = 2. [registry_operation: read_key]
5. Verify registry keys ServerName, ServerPort, AgentVersion, InstallDir under HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\ (x86 uses HKLM\SOFTWARE\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\). [registry_operation: read_key]
6. Verify install dir C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\ exists with bin\, conf\, logs\ subfolders (x86 uses C:\Program Files\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\). [file_folder_operation: check_presence]
7. Verify process DCAgentService.exe is running. [task_manager: verify_process]
8. Verify C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log shows the expected Central Server/DS as the communication target.
Expected Output: Architecture recorded. Machine shows Active in console under the correct office. Service "ManageEngine UEMS Agent Service" RUNNING with Start=2. Registry keys populated with correct ServerName (Central Server IP for Local/Direct, DS IP for Remote-via-DS). Install directory and sub-folders present for the architecture. Process DCAgentService.exe running. DCAgentService.log confirms communication target. Push completed with no interaction on the endpoint; ServerName = Central Server IP.
Support Files: C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log; HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\; C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\; Windows Services (services.msc); UEMS console SoM page (browser)
Remarks: Local Office. Repeat for x86 and ARM64 per matrix.

Title: Push install on Remote Office agent - Direct Communication (x64)
Pre-requisites: Remote Office created with communication type = Direct Communication. Admin creds configured. Target reachable from server over WAN.
Steps:
1. Pre-clean target.
2. Console: Agent > Remote Offices > add/modify the remote office; ensure Direct Communication. [Qengine browser]
3. Add the target via Add Computers and trigger push. [Qengine browser]
1. Record target architecture: run `wmic os get osarchitecture` (use $env:PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE for ARM64). [run_command]
2. In UEMS console (Agent > Scope of Management > Computers) confirm the machine is listed under the correct office with remark "Agent Installed successfully" / status Active. [Qengine browser]
3. Verify service "ManageEngine UEMS Agent Service" exists and is RUNNING. [service_actions: status -> running]
4. Verify startup type Automatic: HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\..\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\DCAgent -> Start = 2. [registry_operation: read_key]
5. Verify registry keys ServerName, ServerPort, AgentVersion, InstallDir under HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\ (x86 uses HKLM\SOFTWARE\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\). [registry_operation: read_key]
6. Verify install dir C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\ exists with bin\, conf\, logs\ subfolders (x86 uses C:\Program Files\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\). [file_folder_operation: check_presence]
7. Verify process DCAgentService.exe is running. [task_manager: verify_process]
8. Verify C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log shows the expected Central Server/DS as the communication target.
Expected Output: Architecture recorded. Machine shows Active in console under the correct office. Service "ManageEngine UEMS Agent Service" RUNNING with Start=2. Registry keys populated with correct ServerName (Central Server IP for Local/Direct, DS IP for Remote-via-DS). Install directory and sub-folders present for the architecture. Process DCAgentService.exe running. DCAgentService.log confirms communication target. Agent registers directly with Central Server (ServerName = Central Server IP); listed under the Remote Office.
Support Files: C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log; HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\; C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\; Windows Services (services.msc); UEMS console SoM page (browser)

Title: Push install on Remote Office agent - via Distribution Server (x64)
Pre-requisites: Remote Office with communication type = Through Distribution Server. DS installed, Active, and has replicated agent binaries. Admin creds configured.
Steps:
1. Pre-clean target.
2. Confirm DS is Active and binaries replicated (DS replication logs). [Qengine browser]
3. Console: add target under the Remote Office and trigger push. [Qengine browser]
1. Record target architecture: run `wmic os get osarchitecture` (use $env:PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE for ARM64). [run_command]
2. In UEMS console (Agent > Scope of Management > Computers) confirm the machine is listed under the correct office with remark "Agent Installed successfully" / status Active. [Qengine browser]
3. Verify service "ManageEngine UEMS Agent Service" exists and is RUNNING. [service_actions: status -> running]
4. Verify startup type Automatic: HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\..\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\DCAgent -> Start = 2. [registry_operation: read_key]
5. Verify registry keys ServerName, ServerPort, AgentVersion, InstallDir under HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\ (x86 uses HKLM\SOFTWARE\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\). [registry_operation: read_key]
6. Verify install dir C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\ exists with bin\, conf\, logs\ subfolders (x86 uses C:\Program Files\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\). [file_folder_operation: check_presence]
7. Verify process DCAgentService.exe is running. [task_manager: verify_process]
8. Verify C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log shows the expected Central Server/DS as the communication target.
Expected Output: Architecture recorded. Machine shows Active in console under the correct office. Service "ManageEngine UEMS Agent Service" RUNNING with Start=2. Registry keys populated with correct ServerName (Central Server IP for Local/Direct, DS IP for Remote-via-DS). Install directory and sub-folders present for the architecture. Process DCAgentService.exe running. DCAgentService.log confirms communication target. ServerName registry value = DS IP (not Central Server). DCAgentService.log shows DS as communication target.
Support Files: C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log; HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\; C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\; Windows Services (services.msc); UEMS console SoM page (browser)

Title: Force push via UEMSRemoteInstaller only (method isolation)
Pre-requisites: Target reachable. Admin creds configured. Access to server/DS bin folder.
Steps:
1. Rename C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\bin\\remcom.exe -> remcom.exe.bak. [file_folder_operation: rename]
2. Rename C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\bin\\remcom64.exe -> remcom64.exe.bak (if present). [file_folder_operation: rename]
3. Stop WMI: `net stop winmgmt /y`. [service_actions: stop]
4. Add target to SoM and trigger push. [Qengine browser]
5. Verify service "ManageEngine UEMS Agent Service" is RUNNING. [service_actions: status]
6. TEARDOWN: `net start winmgmt`; rename remcom.exe.bak/remcom64.exe.bak back to original. [service_actions: start / file_folder_operation: rename]
Expected Output: Push succeeds using only UEMSRemoteInstaller.exe. Agent service RUNNING. Original filenames and WMI restored after the test.
Support Files: C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log; HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\; C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\; Windows Services (services.msc); UEMS console SoM page (browser); C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\bin\\
Remarks: Method isolation. Teardown MUST restore renamed files - RETRY SCENARIO.

Title: Push fallback chain - UEMSRemoteInstaller fails then WMI succeeds
Pre-requisites: Target reachable. Admin creds configured.
Steps:
1. Make UEMSRemoteInstaller path unusable (rename UEMSRemoteInstaller.exe -> .bak). [file_folder_operation: rename]
2. Keep WMI running and remcom present.
3. Trigger push from server. [Qengine browser]
4. Observe server logs: first method fails, fallback proceeds to WMI.
5. Verify "ManageEngine UEMS Agent Service" RUNNING. [service_actions: status]
6. TEARDOWN: restore UEMSRemoteInstaller.exe. [file_folder_operation: rename]
Expected Output: Server attempts UEMSRemoteInstaller -> WMI in order; WMI succeeds; agent installs. Service RUNNING.
Support Files: C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log; HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\; C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\; Windows Services (services.msc); UEMS console SoM page (browser); C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\bin\\
Remarks: Fallback order UEMSRemoteInstaller -> WMI -> remcom. RETRY SCENARIO.

Title: Push install fails with invalid admin credentials
Pre-requisites: Target reachable. Configure incorrect admin credentials in server.
Steps:
1. Configure invalid admin credentials for the target. [Qengine browser]
2. Trigger push install. [Qengine browser]
3. Observe SoM remark.
Expected Output: Push fails with an authentication/access error; SoM remark reflects credential failure. No service/registry created on target.

Title: Push install fails with low-privilege credentials
Pre-requisites: Target reachable. A non-admin (standard) user account available.
Steps:
1. Configure a low-privilege user credential for the target. [Qengine browser]
2. Trigger push install. [Qengine browser]
3. Observe failure.
Expected Output: Push fails (insufficient privilege / network name not available). Agent not installed.

Title: Push fails when File & Printer Sharing / network discovery disabled
Pre-requisites: Target machine with File & Printer Sharing disabled. Valid admin creds.
Steps:
1. On target, disable File and Printer Sharing / network discovery. [control panel - manual]
2. Trigger push install from server. [Qengine browser]
3. Observe error.
Expected Output: Push fails with "network name not available" (or equivalent). Agent not installed.

Title: Push install on unsupported OS (below Windows 7)
Pre-requisites: Target running an OS below Windows 7.
Steps:
1. Trigger push install on the legacy OS target. [Qengine browser]
2. Observe result.
Expected Output: Installation does not succeed; server reports unsupported OS / failure.

Title: Push install on unsupported Windows Server (below 2008 R2)
Pre-requisites: Target running Windows Server below 2008 R2.
Steps:
1. Trigger push install. [Qengine browser]
2. Observe result.
Expected Output: Installation fails; unsupported server OS reported.

Title: Push fails when required ports blocked by firewall
Pre-requisites: Target reachable on network but admin/WMI ports blocked by firewall.
Steps:
1. Block the server-to-target ports / WMI on the target firewall. [manual]
2. Trigger push install. [Qengine browser]
3. Restore firewall after test.
Expected Output: Push fails to connect; appropriate connectivity error shown. Agent not installed.
Support Files: UEMS console SoM remark; target firewall config

Title: Concurrent push to many machines
Pre-requisites: Multiple clean targets reachable. Admin creds configured.
Steps:
1. Add a batch of targets to SoM. [Qengine browser]
2. Trigger push install on all simultaneously. [Qengine browser]
3. Validate each target post-install. 1. Record target architecture: run `wmic os get osarchitecture` (use $env:PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE for ARM64). [run_command]
Expected Output: All targets install successfully without resource contention; each reaches Active with service RUNNING.
Support Files: C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log; HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\; C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\\; Windows Services (services.msc); UEMS console SoM page (browser)

Title: Push fails - DS has not yet replicated binaries (Access Denied)
Pre-requisites: Remote Office via DS. DS freshly added; binary replication NOT yet complete.
Steps:
1. Add a remote office with DS and immediately add a target before replication finishes. [Qengine browser]
2. Trigger push install. [Qengine browser]
3. Observe SoM remark.
4. Wait for DS replication to complete, retry push, and confirm success.
Expected Output: Before replication: push fails with "Access Denied" remark. After replication completes: retry succeeds and agent installs.
Support Files: DS replication logs; UEMS console SoM remark; C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log
Remarks: SUPPORT TICKET: DS-not-replicated-AccessDenied. Timing/race with DS replication.

Title: Push fails - All pipes are busy via UEMSRemoteInstaller
Pre-requisites: Remote Office / Local Office push using UEMSRemoteInstaller under load.
Steps:
1. Drive concurrent push operations through UEMSRemoteInstaller. [Qengine browser]
2. Observe the named-pipe error condition.
3. Verify fallback/retry behavior and final state.
Expected Output: Reproduce "All pipes are busy" from UEMSRemoteInstaller; confirm whether server falls back to WMI/remcom and final install state.
Support Files: UEMS server push logs; C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\bin\\

Title: Push install causes endpoint blackscreen on builds 2540.01-2540.07
Pre-requisites: Target on agent build in the 2540.01-2540.07 range.
Steps:
1. Trigger push install with a build in 2540.01-2540.07. [Qengine browser]
2. Observe the endpoint display after install.
3. Confirm whether the machine goes to blackscreen.
Expected Output: Reproduce the blackscreen on the affected build range; confirm fix on patched builds (no blackscreen, normal logon).
Support Files: Endpoint display; Event Viewer; C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\logs\DCAgentService.log

Title: Pre-install cleanup before push (clean-state baseline)
Pre-requisites: Target may have a stale/previous agent.
Steps:
1. Check whether a UEMS agent is already installed (Control Panel > Programs, service list, HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\). [file_folder_operation/registry_operation]
2. If present, uninstall it (server console Uninstall Agent, or silent: C:\Program Files (x86)\ManageEngine\UEMS_Agent\bin\\DCAgent.exe /uninstall /s). [run_bat]
3. Verify the entry is removed from Control Panel / Apps. [native_gui_operation]
4. Verify service "ManageEngine UEMS Agent Service" is absent or stopped. [service_actions: status]
5. Verify no DCAgentService.exe process is running. [task_manager: verify_process]
6. Verify agent registry keys under HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\AdventNet\DesktopCentral\DCAgent\\ are removed. [registry_operation: read_key]
Expected Output: Machine is in a clean state: no agent in Control Panel, service absent/stopped, no DCAgentService.exe process, agent registry keys removed.
Remarks: Baseline cleanup reused by all install TCs.
